best_extraction.title: La stimulation épidurale dans le traitement des douleurs chroniques
best_extraction.study_question: Can epidural stimulation treat chronic pain in failed disc syndrome patients?
best_extraction.population_assessed: 20 patients with failed disc syndrome after multiple laminectomies
best_extraction.outcome_measures: Pain relief, analgesic use, physical activity, complications
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: Failed disc syndrome after multiple laminectomies; chronic pain requiring treatment
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: Good results in 50%; better for unilateral pain; complications included migration and infection.

reasoning: Anthropic and Google strongly agreed on bibliographic fields, design, year, and sample size. I preferred Anthropic's issue detail and merged both clinical summaries; OpenRouter provided no usable evidence.

field_level_decisions.volume_issue_pages: Used Anthropic's more specific issue notation over Google's volume-only entry.
field_level_decisions.doi: No DOI supplied by reliable models; kept as NAN.
field_level_decisions.study_question: Merged both models' equivalent failed disc syndrome formulations.
field_level_decisions.outcome_measures: Combined shared efficacy outcomes with Anthropic's reported complications.
field_level_decisions.inclusion_criteria: Used overlapping chronic failed-disc criteria, excluding less-certain detoxification detail.
field_level_decisions.answer_to_study_question: Merged consensus 50% benefit with unilateral-pain advantage and noted reported complications.
